growth_records.strain: pure cultures (literature average across strains CBDB1, 195, BAV1, VS; model iAI549)
growth_records.medium: in silico minimal medium (acetate + H2 + CO2 + chlorinated ethene electron acceptor + Cl- + SO4)

up.method: experimental dechlorination rate derived from average pure-culture growth yields and rates (used to constrain the model)
model predicts 67% of biomass carbon from acetate (no experimental numeric rate reported in main text)

sec.method: dechlorination coupled to H2 oxidation; electron-flow rate equivalent to the 10 mmol/gDCW/h H2 uptake, no separate experimental product-secretion number given

mfa.note: experimental dechlorination rate from average pure-culture growth yields/rates; used as model constraint

mfa.reaction_or_metabolite: pyruvate synthesis carbon split (POR / pyruvate synthase)
mfa.note: in silico prediction: 67% of pyruvate carbon from acetate, 33% from CO2 fixed via pyruvate-ferredoxin oxidoreductase (POR); NOT experimental

mfa.reaction_or_metabolite: growth yield on H2 (doubled H2 flux 20 mmol/gDCW/h)
mfa.note: in silico prediction; doubling H2 flux raised predicted yield from 0.72 to 1.19 gDCW/eeq

yields.Yxs: 0.72 gDCW/eeq (electron equivalent) predicted by iAI549 at GAM=61 mmol ATP/gDCW, tuned to reproduce experimental mu=0.014/h; range 0.71-0.72 with/without citrate synthase; model NGAM=1.8 mmol ATP/gDCW/h (taken from other bacteria due to lack of Dehalococcoides chemostat data)

growth_records.cite: Ahsanul Islam M et al. Characterizing the Metabolism of Dehalococcoides with a Constraint-Based Model. PLoS Computational Biology (2010). DOI:10.1371/journal.pcbi.1000887. [PMC2930330]
growth_records.method: reported (average of reported pure-culture experimental growth rates); doubling time calculated
growth_records.snippet: ...used to simulate (using flux balance analysis) the average of reported pure-culture experimental growth rates (0.014 h-1; Table 26 in Text S2). A GAM of 61 mmol ATP/g DCW gave the best pred... a hydrogen flux of 10 mmol.gDCW-1.h-1 (equiv
growth_records.notes: Prokaryote (anaerobic bacterium). Paper is a pan-genome constraint-based (in silico FBA) model (iAI549), NOT a 13C-MFA study; however it explicitly incorporates EXPERIMENTAL prokaryote rates aggregated from prior pure-culture literature: (1) mu = 0.014 h-1 = 'average of reported pure-culture experimental growth rates' (compiled in supplementary Text S2 Table 26; individual source citations not reproduced in main text). Doubling time computed: td = ln2/mu = 0.693/0.014 = 49.5 h. (2) H2 electron-donor uptake = 10 mmol/gDCW/h, stated as 'equivalent to the dechlorination rate obtained from average pure-culture growth yields and rates' / 'corresponding to the experimental dechlorination rate' -> experimental-derived electron donor consumption / reductive dechlorination flux. Predicted (non-experimental) quantities: growth yield 0.72 gDCW/eeq (GAM=61 mmol ATP/gDCW), carbon split 67% acetate / 33% CO2 via POR, yield 1.19 gDCW/eeq at doubled H2 (20 mmol/gDCW/h). Temperature, pH, medium composition not given in main text. Values are literature-averaged across multiple Dehalococcoides pure-culture isolate studies rather than a single strain/condition, hence medium confidence.
